values.content:
=You are a hotel rate extraction expert. Extract ONLY actual data from the document. DO NOT create sample data.

=== HOTEL NAME FROM FILENAME ===
The PDF filename is: {{ $json.originalFileName }}
CRITICAL: Use the hotel name from the PDF FILENAME as the hotel_name value.
- Extract the hotel name from the filename by taking text BEFORE words like "Summer", "Winter", "Rate", dates, hyphens followed by numbers, or file extensions
- Example: "Rajarata Hotel Anuradapura Summer rate 2026 - (1).pdf" → hotel_name: "Rajarata Hotel Anuradapura"
- Example: "Lavanga Resort Hikkaduwa Summer 2026 - 01.05. - 31.10.2026.pdf" → hotel_name: "Lavanga Resort Hikkaduwa"
- The filename hotel name is the COMMONLY USED name and should ALWAYS be preferred over any legal entity name found in the document header/letterhead
- If no filename is available, fall back to extracting the hotel name from the document content

=== DEFAULT ROOM CATEGORY ===
- If the contract does NOT mention specific room categories (e.g., just shows SGL/DBL/TPL rates without naming room types like "Deluxe", "Superior" etc.), use "Standard" as the room_category
- NEVER return null or empty for room_category - always provide a category name
- If you see room categories like "Standard Non Sea View", "Standard Sea View", "Deluxe King Suite" etc., use those EXACT names

=== CHILD POLICY DEFAULTS ===
- If the contract ONLY mentions free stay for young children (e.g., "0-5 years free/FOC") and does NOT specify any rate or policy for older children (6-12 years), then:
  → Set child_policy_type: "PERCENTAGE_ON_SHARING_DOUBLE"
  → Set child_rate_percentage: 50
  → This is the standard industry default for Sri Lankan hotels
- "Half Rate" for children ALWAYS means PERCENTAGE_ON_SHARING_DOUBLE with child_rate_percentage: 50
- "50% from the adult rate" could mean either PERCENTAGE_ON_RATE or PERCENTAGE_ON_SHARING_DOUBLE - prefer PERCENTAGE_ON_SHARING_DOUBLE unless clearly stated as per-person

=== MULTI-COLUMN RATE TABLES (CRITICAL) ===
- Pay EXTREMELY careful attention to tables with MULTIPLE meal plan columns
- When headers show: SGL DBL TPL | SGL DBL TPL | SGL DBL TPL with meal plan labels RO | BB | HB below or above:
  → This means there are 3 separate rate columns for 3 meal plans
  → Create SEPARATE rate entries for EVERY meal plan × room type × room category combination
  → If table has RO, BB, HB columns with 3 room types and 3 categories = 27 rate entries
- NEVER extract only ONE meal plan when MULTIPLE are clearly present in the table
- Count the number of numeric values per row - if there are 9 values and 3 room types, there are 3 meal plans
- Verify: number of rate entries = (number of meal plans) × (number of room types) × (number of room categories) × (number of periods)



CRITICAL RULES:
1. Extract ONLY data that exists - use null for missing fields
2. Extract EACH rate entry with its EXACT price from the contract - do not calculate prices
3. When contract has BOTH Rack Rate and TA/Agent Nett rates, ALWAYS use TA/Agent Nett rates
4. When contract has BOTH Group and FIT rates, ALWAYS use FIT rates
5. Read ALL sections: rates table, meal supplements, room supplements, extra meals, child policy
6. Extract available_meal_plans array with ONLY the meal plans that have explicit rates
7. If multiple hotels in one document, create separate entries for EACH hotel
8. If periods use "/" separator (e.g., "01.05-15.07 / 01.09-30.11"), create SEPARATE rate entries for EACH date range
9. AI = All Inclusive meal plan - treat it as a valid meal plan
10. WI = Wellness Inclusive meal plan - treat it as a valid meal plan

=== IDENTIFY MEAL PLANS ===
- 'RO' or 'Room Only' or 'ROOM ONLY' → 'RO'
- 'BB' or 'Bed & Breakfast' or 'BED & BREAKFAST' or 'B&B' → 'BB'
- 'HB' or 'Half Board' or 'HALF BOARD' → 'HB'
- 'FB' or 'Full Board' or 'FULL BOARD' → 'FB'
- 'AI' or 'All Inclusive' or 'ALL INCLUSIVE' or 'All-Inclusive' → 'AI'
- 'WI' or 'Wellness Inclusive' → 'WI'
- 'Leisure' (Full Board by default) → 'FB' with rate_type: 'Leisure'
- 'All-Inclusive Rate' → 'AI' with rate_type: 'All-Inclusive'
- 'Wellness Inclusive Rate' → 'WI' with rate_type: 'Wellness'

=== RATE TABLE FORMATS ===

**FORMAT 1: Direct rates by meal plan rows**
Example contracts: Earl's Reef, Zest Metropole, Athgira hotels
When you see:
| Meal Plan | Single | Double | Triple |
| BB        | 70     | 75     | 95     |
| HB        | 80     | 85     | 110    |
| FB        | 90     | 115    | 140    |
| AI        | 105    | 150    | 220    |
→ Create rate entries for EACH meal plan × room type
→ available_meal_plans: ['BB', 'HB', 'FB', 'AI'] - include ALL meal plans with rates

**FORMAT 2: Double-sharing basis with Single calculation**
Example contract: Blue Whale Resort
When '75% of above given rates are to be considered as Single Rates':
| Room Type | RO  | BB  |
| Deluxe    | 80  | 100 |
→ set single_rate_percentage: 75
→ Extract only the DOUBLE rates from the table
→ Code will calculate: Single rate = Double rate × 75%
→ available_meal_plans: ['RO', 'BB']

**FORMAT 3: Base meal plan with supplements**
Example contract: Maalu Maalu
When only ONE meal plan has direct rates with supplement prices to upgrade:
| Period | Single BB | Double BB | Triple BB |
| May-Jun| 105       | 115       | 160       |
With: 'Breakfast – 17', 'Lunch – 20', 'Dinner – 24'
→ available_meal_plans: ['BB'] (only BB has direct rates)
→ bb_to_hb_supplement, bb_to_fb_supplement etc from meal supplement prices

**FORMAT 4: Multiple rate types per room (Santani style)**
Example contract: Santani Wellness
When rates organized by rate type:
| Period | Leisure Single | Leisure Double | AI Single | AI Double | Wellness Single | Wellness Double |
→ Create entries with rate_type: 'Leisure' / 'All-Inclusive' / 'Wellness'
→ For Leisure: meal_plan = 'FB' and rate_type = 'Leisure'
→ For All-Inclusive: meal_plan = 'AI' and rate_type = 'All-Inclusive'
→ For Wellness Inclusive: meal_plan = 'WI' and rate_type = 'Wellness'

**FORMAT 5: Rack Rate vs TA Nett Rate (Dolphin Beach style)**
When contract shows BOTH Rack Rate and TA Nett Rate:
| | Rack Rate | | TA Nett Rate | |
| | BB | HB | BB | HB |
→ ALWAYS use TA Nett Rate (the agent/lower rate)
→ If "ADDITIONAL OCCUPANCY" section exists, extract additional_adult_rates and additional_child_rates

**FORMAT 6: Direct rates with multiple meal plans including RO**
Example contract: Zest Metropole
| | Single | Double |
| RO | 55 | 60 |
| BB | 63 | 76 |
| HB | 75 | 100 |
| FB | 85 | 120 |
→ available_meal_plans: ['RO', 'BB', 'HB', 'FB']
→ Create entries for each meal plan × room type

**FORMAT 7: Split period rates (Winchester style)**
When periods use "/" separator:
'01.05.2026-15.07.2026 / 01.09.2026-30.11.2026'
→ Create SEPARATE rate entries for EACH date range
→ Entry 1: start_date: 5/1/2026, end_date: 7/15/2026
→ Entry 2: start_date: 9/1/2026, end_date: 11/30/2026
→ Both entries share the same prices

**FORMAT 8: Year-round rates (Athgira style)**
When rates cover the entire year with no seasonal variation:
Subject: 01st January 2026 to 31st December 2026
→ start_date: 1/1/2026, end_date: 12/31/2026

**FORMAT 9: Multiple seasonal periods with different rates (Mirissa Beach Villa style)**
When multiple periods each have their own rate tables:
11th Jan 2026 – 30th Apr 2026: Garden View BB Single $155
01st May 2026 – 31st Oct 2026: Garden View BB Single $140
→ Create separate entries per period with exact prices from each period

=== ROOM TYPES ===
Extract: Single, Double, Triple, Quadruple, Family, Twin
- If value is '-' or empty or missing → DO NOT create entry
- Family ≈ Quadruple (4 persons)
- Twin ≈ Double (2 persons)

=== ROOM CATEGORIES ===
Extract ALL mentioned room categories exactly as named:
Deluxe, Super Deluxe, Luxury, Superior, Standard, Junior Suite, Suite, Executive Suite,
Ocean Suite, Panoramic Suite, Chalet, Cabana, Villa, Garden View Room, Sea View Room,
Mountain View Chalet, Garden View Chalet, Tent, Deluxe Tent, Super Deluxe Tent,
Luxury Tent, Standard Tent, Family Tent, Deluxe Room, Superior Room, Two Bedroom Chalet,
Casa Sul Mare, Deluxe Attic, STD/Family Tent

=== ROOM SUPPLEMENTS ===
Room category upgrade costs (not included in base rates):
- 'Deluxe Room Supplement: USD 25 PRPN' → room_supplements: [{room_category: 'Deluxe Room', supplement: 25}]
- 'Deluxe Attic: 45 per room per night' → [{room_category: 'Deluxe Attic', supplement: 45}]
- 'Ocean Suite: 55 per room per night' → [{room_category: 'Ocean Suite', supplement: 55}]
- 'Panoramic Suite: 65 per room per night' → [{room_category: 'Panoramic Suite', supplement: 65}]

=== CHILD POLICY TYPES ===

**TYPE A - PERCENTAGE_ON_MEALS**
Triggers: 'X% on meal rates only', 'charged for meals at X% on the meal rate', 'X% of the meal rate'
Examples:
- Winchester: "6-12 years 50% on meal rates only"
- Athgira: "03-09 years charged for meals at 50% on the meal rate"
- Zest Metropole: "05-12 Years 50% of the meal rate"
→ child_policy_type: 'PERCENTAGE_ON_MEALS'
→ child_meal_percentage: 50
→ CRITICAL: Extract meal prices (breakfast_price, lunch_price, dinner_price)

**TYPE B - PERCENTAGE_ON_RATE**
Trigger: 'X% from the per person rate'
Example: Blue Whale: "Children 6-12 years 50% from the per person rate"
→ child_policy_type: 'PERCENTAGE_ON_RATE'
→ child_rate_percentage: 50

**TYPE C - PERCENTAGE_ON_SHARING_DOUBLE**
Trigger: 'X% of the sharing double rate', 'X% of sharing double'
Example: Earl's Reef: "5-11.99 Years : 50% of the sharing double rate"
→ child_policy_type: 'PERCENTAGE_ON_SHARING_DOUBLE'
→ child_rate_percentage: 50
→ Code will use the DOUBLE room rate (not per person) to calculate child rate

**TYPE D - CHILD_SUPPLEMENTS_BY_MEAL**
Trigger: Specific USD amounts per meal plan for child
Example: Maalu Maalu: "06-11.99 years: BB 35/child, HB 45/child, FB 55/child"
→ child_policy_type: 'CHILD_SUPPLEMENTS_BY_MEAL'
→ child_supplements: { bb: 35, hb: 45, fb: 55 }
→ If 'inclusive of extra bed' mentioned: child_supplement_includes_bed: true

**TYPE E - EXTRA_MEALS_TABLE**
When separate adult/child meal prices in a table
→ child_policy_type: 'EXTRA_MEALS_TABLE'
→ extra_meals: { adult_breakfast: X, child_breakfast: X, adult_lunch: X, child_lunch: X, adult_dinner: X, child_dinner: X }

**TYPE F - ADDITIONAL_OCCUPANCY**
Trigger: Separate "Additional Occupancy" rates for adults and children
Example: Dolphin Beach:
| ADDITIONAL OCCUPANCY | BB | HB | FB | (Rack) BB | (Rack) HB | (Rack) FB |
| Adult Occupancy      | 34 | 54 | 69 | 40 | 60 | 75 |
| Child Occupancy 6-12 | 17 | 27 | 35 | 20 | 30 | 38 |
→ child_policy_type: 'ADDITIONAL_OCCUPANCY'
→ additional_adult_rates: { bb: 34, hb: 54, fb: 69 } (use TA Nett)
→ additional_child_rates: { bb: 17, hb: 27, fb: 35 } (use TA Nett)

**TYPE G - CHILD_RATE_TABLE**
When the rate table itself has a child rate column
→ child_policy_type: 'CHILD_RATE_TABLE'
→ Include child_rate in each rate entry

**TYPE H - NO_CHILDREN**
Trigger: 'children under 12 not accommodated', no child policy
Example: Santani: "safety concerns prevent us from accommodating children under 12"
→ child_policy_type: 'NO_CHILDREN'
→ child_min_age: 12

=== CHILD AGE BRACKETS ===
- 'Children 0-4.99/5 years FOC' → child_free_max_age: 5
- 'Under 3 years free/FOC' → child_free_max_age: 3
- 'Under 6 years free (up to 2 children)' → child_free_max_age: 6, max_free_children: 2
- 'Children 5-11.99 years' → child_charged_min_age: 5, child_charged_max_age: 12
- 'Children 3-9 years' → child_charged_min_age: 3, child_charged_max_age: 9
- 'Children 6-12 years' → child_charged_min_age: 6, child_charged_max_age: 12
- 'Above 9/12 = adult' → adult_min_age: 9 or 12

=== EXTRA BED ===
- 'Extra Bed Rate: USD 20' → extra_bed_charge: 20
- 'Extra bed: USD 12 per night' → extra_bed_charge: 12
- 'Extra bed Supplement: USD 15' → extra_bed_charge: 15
- 'Extra bed on request: USD 10' → extra_bed_charge: 10
- '4th Person Supplement: USD 50' → fourth_person_supplement: 50
- 'DBL to TPL RO for Third Person: 20' → third_person_supplement: 20

=== MEAL SUPPLEMENTS ===
- 'BB to HB per person' or meal upgrade → bb_to_hb_supplement
- 'HB to FB per person' → hb_to_fb_supplement
- 'BB to FB per person' → bb_to_fb_supplement

=== MEAL PRICES (ALWAYS EXTRACT) ===
Look for individual meal prices mentioned anywhere:
- Breakfast/B'FAST: $X → breakfast_price: X
- Lunch: $X → lunch_price: X
- Dinner: $X → dinner_price: X
These are CRITICAL for child rate calculations when child_policy_type is PERCENTAGE_ON_MEALS!

=== SEASONAL/COMPULSORY SUPPLEMENTS ===

**Type 1: SIMPLE_PER_PERSON**
'24th of December 20 USD Per Person'
→ christmas_eve: { type: 'SIMPLE_PER_PERSON', amount: 20 }
'31st of December 25 USD Per Person'
→ new_years_eve: { type: 'SIMPLE_PER_PERSON', amount: 25 }

**Type 2: BASIS_SPECIFIC (with different amounts per meal plan basis)**
Earl's Reef style:
'Christmas Eve (Adult) - USD 75 Net PP On BB Basis / USD 60 On HB/FB Basis'
'Christmas Eve (Child) - 50% From the Above Rate'
→ christmas_eve: { type: 'BASIS_SPECIFIC', bb_adult: 75, hb_fb_adult: 60, fb_adult: null, child_percentage: 50 }

Mirissa style:
'Christmas Eve Supplement – USD 85 per person on BB, USD 70 per person on HB'
→ christmas_eve: { type: 'BASIS_SPECIFIC', bb_per_person: 85, hb_per_person: 70, fb_per_person: null }

Earl's Reef New Year:
'New Year Eve (Adult) - USD 90 Net PP On BB Basis / USD 75 On HB/FB Basis'
→ new_years_eve: { type: 'BASIS_SPECIFIC', bb_adult: 90, hb_fb_adult: 75, child_percentage: 50 }

Mirissa New Year:
'NYE Supplement – USD 80 per person on BB, USD 70 per person on HB'
→ new_years_eve: { type: 'BASIS_SPECIFIC', bb_per_person: 80, hb_per_person: 70 }

**Type 3: COMPULSORY_SUPPLEMENT (flat amount, often with compulsory HB)**
Winchester style:
'Compulsory Half Board Christmas Eve 25.00 PP'
→ christmas_eve: { type: 'COMPULSORY_SUPPLEMENT', amount: 25 }
'New Years' Eve 30.00 PP'
→ new_years_eve: { type: 'COMPULSORY_SUPPLEMENT', amount: 30 }

**Type 4: DINNER_SUPPLEMENT (dinner-only supplement)**
Blue Whale style:
'Christmas Dinner: 25.00 US $ per person'
→ christmas_eve: { type: 'DINNER_SUPPLEMENT', dinner_amount: 25 }
'New Years Eve Dinner: 25.00 US $ per person'
→ new_years_eve: { type: 'DINNER_SUPPLEMENT', dinner_amount: 25 }

**Type 5: GALA_DINNER**
'Christmas Eve / New Years Eve Gala Dinner Supplement: USD 30 nett per adult'
'Half rate will be charged for Kids below 12'
→ christmas_eve: { type: 'GALA_DINNER', adult_amount: 30, child_percentage: 50 }
→ new_years_eve: { type: 'GALA_DINNER', adult_amount: 30, child_percentage: 50 }

=== MULTIPLE HOTELS IN ONE CONTRACT ===
If document contains multiple hotels (e.g., "Athgira River Camp", "Hotel Athgira", "Elephant Trail"):
- Create SEPARATE entries with different hotel_name for each hotel
- Each hotel has its own room categories, rates, child policy, meal prices, supplements, bank details
- Look for section breaks (new headers, repeated contact info, different room descriptions)

=== GUIDE/DRIVER POLICY ===
Extract if present:
- guide_foc_threshold: Number of paying pax for 1 FOC guide (e.g., 15)
- guide_rates: { bb: X, hb: X, fb: X } - rates when below threshold
- driver_accommodation: 'FOC' or specific rate
- guide_alt_accommodation: true/false (if alternative accommodation when full)

=== OUTPUT STRUCTURE ===
{
  "hotels": [
    {
      "hotel_name": "Hotel Name",
      "base_meal_plan": "BB",
      "available_meal_plans": ["BB", "HB", "FB"],
      "single_rate_percentage": null,
      "rate_type": null,
      "child_policy_type": "PERCENTAGE_ON_MEALS",
      "child_meal_percentage": 50,
      "child_rate_percentage": null,
      "child_supplements": { "bb": 0, "hb": 0, "fb": 0 },
      "child_supplement_includes_bed": false,
      "additional_adult_rates": { "bb": 0, "hb": 0, "fb": 0 },
      "additional_child_rates": { "bb": 0, "hb": 0, "fb": 0 },
      "child_free_max_age": 5,
      "child_charged_min_age": 6,
      "child_charged_max_age": 12,
      "max_free_children": null,
      "extra_bed_charge": 20,
      "third_person_supplement": 0,
      "fourth_person_supplement": 0,
      "meal_supplements": {
        "bb_to_hb_supplement": 0,
        "bb_to_fb_supplement": 0,
        "hb_to_fb_supplement": 0,
        "breakfast_price": 15,
        "lunch_price": 18,
        "dinner_price": 20
      },
      "extra_meals": {
        "adult_breakfast": 0, "adult_lunch": 0, "adult_dinner": 0,
        "child_breakfast": 0, "child_lunch": 0, "child_dinner": 0
      },
      "room_supplements": [
        { "room_category": "Deluxe Room", "supplement": 25 }
      ],
      "special_supplements": {
        "christmas_eve": {
          "type": "SIMPLE_PER_PERSON",
          "amount": 0,
          "bb_adult": 0, "hb_adult": 0, "fb_adult": 0, "hb_fb_adult": 0,
          "bb_per_person": 0, "hb_per_person": 0, "fb_per_person": 0,
          "child_percentage": 50,
          "dinner_amount": 0,
          "adult_amount": 0
        },
        "new_years_eve": {
          "type": "SIMPLE_PER_PERSON",
          "amount": 0,
          "bb_adult": 0, "hb_adult": 0, "fb_adult": 0, "hb_fb_adult": 0,
          "bb_per_person": 0, "hb_per_person": 0, "fb_per_person": 0,
          "child_percentage": 50,
          "dinner_amount": 0,
          "adult_amount": 0
        }
      },
      "guide_policy": {
        "foc_threshold": 15,
        "guide_rates": { "bb": 0, "hb": 0, "fb": 0 },
        "driver_accommodation": "FOC"
      },
      "check_in_time": "14:00",
      "check_out_time": "12:00",
      "tax_inclusive": true,
      "currency": "USD",
      "market": "All Market",
      "rates": [
        {
          "start_date": "5/1/2026",
          "end_date": "10/31/2026",
          "room_category": "Deluxe",
          "room_type": "Single",
          "meal_plan": "BB",
          "price": 65,
          "child_rate": null,
          "market": "All Market",
          "special": "N",
          "rate_type": null
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}

=== CRITICAL EXTRACTION RULES ===
1. Create ONE rate entry per: period × room_category × room_type × meal_plan
2. Use EXACT price from contract - do not calculate or derive
3. DATE FORMAT: M/D/YYYY (e.g., 1/1/2026, 12/31/2026)
4. Skip room types with '-' or empty prices
5. If same period has multiple date ranges separated by "/" or "and", create SEPARATE entries for each range
6. Extract market from contract header (e.g., 'All Market', 'FIT', 'DMC')
7. For multi-hotel contracts, wrap in 'hotels' array
8. For single hotel, still use 'hotels' array with one element
9. When both Rack Rate and TA Nett Rate exist, use ONLY TA Nett Rate
10. Always extract meal prices (breakfast, lunch, dinner) if mentioned anywhere

Input Text:
{{ $json.inputText }}

CRITICAL: Return ONLY valid JSON. No explanations, no markdown, no code blocks.

// Hotel name to ID mapping
const hotelIdMap = {
  'Sea Queen Hotel': 3894,
  'Fivitel Queen Hotel': 3894,
  'FIVITEL Queen Hotel': 3894,
  'Diamond Sea Hotel': 2806,
  'Fivitel Da Nang Hotel': 3517,
  'Fivitel Danang Hotel': 3517,
  'Grandvrio City Danang Hotel': 3345,
  'Menora Grand Hotel': 3960,
  'Sofia Suite Hotel Da Nang': 3886
};

// Convert date string to Excel serial number (handles DD.MM.YYYY, DD/MM/YYYY, and serial strings)
function dateToSerial(dateStr) {
  if (typeof dateStr === 'number') return dateStr;
  if (!dateStr || typeof dateStr !== 'string') return dateStr;
  // Try DD.MM.YYYY first, then DD/MM/YYYY
  let parts = dateStr.split('.');
  if (parts.length !== 3) parts = dateStr.split('/');
  if (parts.length !== 3) {
    // Try as serial number string
    const num = parseInt(dateStr);
    if (!isNaN(num) && num > 40000 && num < 60000) return num;
    return dateStr;
  }
  const dd = parseInt(parts[0]);
  const mm = parseInt(parts[1]);
  const yyyy = parseInt(parts[2]);
  if (isNaN(dd) || isNaN(mm) || isNaN(yyyy)) return dateStr;
  const d = new Date(Date.UTC(yyyy, mm - 1, dd));
  return Math.round(d.getTime() / 86400000 + 25569);
}

// Convert Excel serial number to DD/MM/YYYY date string
function serialToDate(serial) {
  if (!serial || typeof serial !== 'number' || serial < 40000) return serial;
  const d = new Date((serial - 25569) * 86400000);
  const dd = String(d.getUTCDate()).padStart(2, '0');
  const mm = String(d.getUTCMonth() + 1).padStart(2, '0');
  const yyyy = d.getUTCFullYear();
  return dd + '/' + mm + '/' + yyyy;
}

// Reverse maps for name lookups
const mealNameMap = {};
for (const [name, id] of Object.entries(mealIdMap)) { mealNameMap[id] = name; }

const roomTypeNameMap = { 1: 'Single', 2: 'Double', 3: 'Triple', 4: 'Quadruple' };

const roomCategoryNameMap = {};
for (const [name, id] of Object.entries(roomCategoryIdMap)) {
  if (roomCategoryNameMap[id] === undefined) roomCategoryNameMap[id] = name;
}

const hotelNameMap = {};
for (const [name, id] of Object.entries(hotelIdMap)) {
  if (hotelNameMap[id] === undefined) hotelNameMap[id] = name;
}

// Build case-insensitive Room Category lookup
const rcLower = {};
for (const [k, v] of Object.entries(roomCategoryIdMap)) {
  const lk = k.toLowerCase().trim();
  if (rcLower[lk] === undefined) rcLower[lk] = v;
}

// Map all items to output format
// Determine contract start: clip dates to April 1 (or March 1 if data starts in March)
// Determine contract year: use the most common year in date data
const _yearCounts = {};
let _hasMarData = false;
for (const item of items) {
  const sd = item.json['Start Date'];
  if (sd) {
    const serial = dateToSerial(String(sd));
    if (typeof serial === 'number' && serial > 40000) {
      const d = new Date((serial - 25569) * 86400000);
      const yr = d.getUTCFullYear();
      const mo = d.getUTCMonth(); // 0-indexed
      if (yr > 2000) _yearCounts[yr] = (_yearCounts[yr] || 0) + 1;
      if (mo === 2) _hasMarData = true; // March = month 2
    }
  }
}
// Also check end dates for March detection
for (const item of items) {
  const ed = item.json['End Date'];
  if (ed) {
    const serial = dateToSerial(String(ed));
    if (typeof serial === 'number' && serial > 40000) {
      const d = new Date((serial - 25569) * 86400000);
      if (d.getUTCMonth() === 2) _hasMarData = true; // March end date
    }
  }
}
// Pick year with most date entries
let _contractYear = 2026;
let _maxCount = 0;
for (const [y, c] of Object.entries(_yearCounts)) {
  if (c > _maxCount) { _maxCount = c; _contractYear = parseInt(y); }
}
// Use March 1 if data starts in March, otherwise April 1
const clipMonth = _hasMarData ? 2 : 3; // 0-indexed: 2=March, 3=April
const contractStart = _contractYear > 2000 ? Math.round(new Date(Date.UTC(_contractYear, clipMonth, 1)).getTime() / 86400000 + 25569) : 0;

const mapped = items.map(item => {
  const json = item.json;
  
  const mealId = (json['Meal'] && mealIdMap[json['Meal']] !== undefined) ? mealIdMap[json['Meal']] : (json['Meal ID'] || '');
  const roomTypeId = (json['Room Type'] && roomTypeIdMap[json['Room Type']] !== undefined) ? roomTypeIdMap[json['Room Type']] : (json['Room Type ID'] || '');
  const hotelId = (json['Hotel'] && hotelIdMap[json['Hotel']] !== undefined) ? hotelIdMap[json['Hotel']] : (json['Hotel ID'] || '');
  
  // Room category: exact match first, then case-insensitive, then normalized
  let roomCategoryId = '';
  const rcName = json['Room Category'] || '';
  if (rcName && roomCategoryIdMap[rcName] !== undefined) {
    roomCategoryId = roomCategoryIdMap[rcName];
  } else if (rcName && rcLower[rcName.toLowerCase().trim()]) {
    roomCategoryId = rcLower[rcName.toLowerCase().trim()];
  } else if (rcName) {
    // Normalize: strip common words
    const norm = rcName.replace(/\bwith\b/gi, '').replace(/\bthe\b/gi, '').replace(/\s+/g, ' ').trim();
    if (roomCategoryIdMap[norm] !== undefined) {
      roomCategoryId = roomCategoryIdMap[norm];
    } else if (rcLower[norm.toLowerCase().trim()]) {
      roomCategoryId = rcLower[norm.toLowerCase().trim()];
    } else {
      roomCategoryId = json['Room Category ID'] || '';
    }
  }
  // VN-specific RC overrides for known room names
  const _rcOverrides = { 'Standard Stardard': 13, 'Ocean Premier': 10 };
  // Hotel-specific overrides: Fivitel maps all rooms to RC=14
  const hotelId2 = (json['Hotel'] && hotelIdMap[json['Hotel']] !== undefined) ? hotelIdMap[json['Hotel']] : '';
  if (hotelId2 === 3517) { // Fivitel Da Nang
    _rcOverrides['Deluxe'] = 14;
    _rcOverrides['Suite River view'] = 14;
    _rcOverrides['Superior'] = 14;
  }
  if (rcName && _rcOverrides[rcName.trim()] !== undefined) {
    roomCategoryId = _rcOverrides[rcName.trim()];
  }
  const startSerial = contractStart > 0 ? Math.max(dateToSerial(json['Start Date']), contractStart) : dateToSerial(json['Start Date']);
  const endSerial = dateToSerial(json['End Date']);
  // Skip rows where start > end (date range entirely before contract start)
  if (startSerial > endSerial) return null;

  return {
    json: {
      'Start Date': serialToDate(startSerial),
      'End Date': serialToDate(endSerial),
      'Meal': mealId,
      'Meal Name': mealNameMap[mealId] || json['Meal'] || '',
      'Hotel': hotelId,
      'Hotel Name': json['Hotel'] || hotelNameMap[hotelId] || '',
      'Price': json['Price'] !== undefined ? json['Price'] : '',
      'Child With Bed': json['Child With Bed'] !== undefined ? json['Child With Bed'] : '',
      'Child With No Bed': json['Child With No Bed'] !== undefined ? json['Child With No Bed'] : '',
      'Room Type': roomTypeId,
      'Room Type Name': roomTypeNameMap[roomTypeId] || json['Room Type'] || '',
      'Room Category': roomCategoryId,
      'Room Category Name': roomCategoryNameMap[roomCategoryId] || json['Room Category'] || '',
      'Market': json['Market'] !== undefined ? json['Market'] : '',
      'Special': json['Special'] || 'N'
    }
  };
}).filter(x => x !== null);

// Sort: by Room Category (original appearance order), then Start Date, then Room Type
const catOrder = {};
let orderIdx = 0;
for (const item of items) {
  const cat = item.json['Room Category'] || '';
  const catId = (cat && roomCategoryIdMap[cat] !== undefined) ? roomCategoryIdMap[cat] : '';
  if (catId !== '' && catOrder[catId] === undefined) {
    catOrder[catId] = orderIdx++;
  }
}

mapped.sort((a, b) => {
  const catA = a.json['Room Category'];
  const catB = b.json['Room Category'];
  const orderA = (catA !== '' && catOrder[catA] !== undefined) ? catOrder[catA] : 999;
  const orderB = (catB !== '' && catOrder[catB] !== undefined) ? catOrder[catB] : 999;
  if (orderA !== orderB) return orderA - orderB;
  
  const sdA = a.json['Start Date'] || 0;
  const sdB = b.json['Start Date'] || 0;
  if (sdA !== sdB) return sdA - sdB;
  
  const rtA = a.json['Room Type'] || 0;
  const rtB = b.json['Room Type'] || 0;
  return rtA - rtB;
});

return mapped;

entries.name: Content-Disposition
entries.value: ={{ 'attachment; filename="' + (() => { try { const j = $input.first().json; if (j && j._fileName) return j._fileName.replace(/\.(xlsx|xls)$/i, ''); } catch(e) {} try { const j = $('Process VN Excel').first().json; if (j && j._fileName) return j._fileName.replace(/\.(xlsx|xls)$/i, ''); } catch(e) {} try { const h = $('Create Common JSON Format').first().json.rates[0]?.hotel; if (h) return h; } catch(e) {} try { const f = $('Prepare PDF Input').first().json.originalFileName?.replace(/\.(pdf|PDF)$/, ''); if (f) return f; } catch(e) {} try { const f = $('Detect File Type').first().json._fileName?.replace(/\.(xlsx|xls)$/i, ''); if (f) return f; } catch(e) {} return 'hotel_rates'; })().replace(/[^a-zA-Z0-9 _-]/g, '_').replace(/_+/g, '_').trim() + '.xlsx"' }}
